title: tanacetum abrotanoides
intro: Tanacetum abrotanoides, also known as camphor daisy or white daisy, is a flowering plant in the daisy family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include being antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant. The plant's roots and leaves have been used to treat various health issues, including colds, fever, and digestive problems, as well as to relieve pain and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, Tanacetum abrotanoides is often combined with other herbs to create teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to alleviate respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
